text:
Department of Neurology

Clinic Date: 07 October 2025

Dr A. Rahman
St Cuthbert’s Neurology Centre
Outpatients, 12 Fernbank Way, Manchester M14 3QT

Dear Dr A. Rahman,

Michael Turner, DOB: 23-02-1989, Hospital No: H392781 NHS No. 9065174421
Flat 5, 28 Brookfield Avenue, Manchester, M20 6LP

Diagnoses:

Primary generalised epilepsy with generalised spike-and-wave discharges on EEG (most recently documented January 2025)

Current Medication:

Levetiracetam 1,000 mg twice daily
Clobazam 10 mg nocte (as required for clustering)

Plan:

- Continue levetiracetam 1,000 mg twice daily
- Use clobazam 10 mg at night for up to 3 nights if clusters occur
- Safety counselling given including SUDEP discussion, sleep hygiene, and alcohol moderation
- Arrange repeat EEG in 12 months and routine follow-up in 6 months

I reviewed Michael Turner in clinic today regarding ongoing seizure control. He reports that his typical events are generalised tonic–clonic seizures, preceded by a brief warning of sudden light-headedness and blurred awareness, followed by a witnessed convulsion with tongue biting and post-ictal confusion lasting 30–60 minutes. He and his partner have kept a seizure diary which indicates the seizures are occurring every 4 days on average over the past two months. This pattern is consistent with his known diagnosis of generalised epilepsy. He denies clear focal onset or lateralised features, and there is no consistent positional trigger. Sleep loss and missed doses appear to lower the threshold.

There is no history of status epilepticus. He has not sustained serious injuries, though there was one fall in the bathroom without head trauma. He is currently not driving and understands that, given the current frequency of seizures occurring every 4 days, he does not meet DVLA standards for driving. He has reduced alcohol intake and is working on regular sleep routine. He reports no adverse effects on levetiracetam beyond mild irritability, which is manageable. Clobazam has been used sparingly for brief clusters with some benefit.

Examination today is unremarkable. Routine blood tests from last month (U&Es, LFTs, FBC, vitamin D) were within normal limits. The most recent EEG demonstrated generalised spike-and-wave discharges without focal abnormalities, supporting the diagnosis of generalised epilepsy. MRI brain (2023) was normal.

We discussed options to improve control. Given ongoing seizures every 4 days, I have advised strict adherence to medication timing and sleep hygiene, and we agreed to continue the current dose of levetiracetam for now while we complete a medication adherence check and consider an add-on agent at the next review if the pattern persists. A rescue plan with clobazam for short clusters has been provided. Seizure first-aid and lifestyle precautions were reinforced. We will reassess in six months, sooner if there is any escalation.

Please do not hesitate to contact me if further information is required.

With best wishes,

Yours sincerely

entities.text: the seizures are occurring every 4 days on average over the past two months

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly states the patient's seizures are occurring every 4 days on average over the past two months, and reiterates this frequency later. No other conflicting frequencies are provided. Therefore, select the highest (and only) frequency stated: 1 per 4 days.
seizure_frequency_number: the seizures are occurring every 4 days on average over the past two months
